header: Multi-camera video recording for educational and research use.
footer: © 2026–Present Aung Ye Mun and contributors. Non-commercial research and educational use only.
videoSettingsApplying: Applying resolution and FPS to cameras…
videoSettingsApplied: Resolution and FPS updated for preview and recording.
videoSettingsWhileRecordingHint: Stop recording before changing resolution or FPS.
calibEnvLockReleased: Environmental lock released. Auto modes restored.
calibCameraNotOpen: Camera not open on this slot.
calibOneShotWaiting: Auto modes active — waiting 2.5 s for ISP convergence...
lockEnvironmentalSettingsTooltip: Disables all auto modes (focus, exposure, white balance, ISO) and reads the frozen hardware register values into the sliders above. Press once arena lighting and the calibration target are in place.
oneShotAutoCalibrateTooltip: Turns on all auto modes, waits 2.5 s for ISP convergence, then freezes the settled hardware values. Recommended at the start of each new lighting condition.
fpsBoxTooltip: 15/30 fps — standard scientific capture. 60 fps — high-speed; requires a camera that supports the rate and sufficient USB bandwidth. MultiCamApp uses FindKnownVideoProfiles to detect recording-specific high-FPS modes.
focusExposureScopeNote:
Focus and exposure settings apply to the selected camera only.
Each camera can have independent values.
autoFocusTooltip: Some cameras ignore software autofocus commands. If focus still changes, disable autofocus in the camera body or vendor software.
manualFocusDisabledWhileAuto: Manual focus is disabled while autofocus is enabled.
manualFocusUnavailable: Manual focus unavailable for this camera/driver.
manualFocusBestEffort: Manual focus is best-effort; unsupported cameras may ignore this value.
defaultFocusButtonTooltip: Restore this camera's focus to its research-safe default (autofocus off / fixed focus mode). Clears any manual focus override.
focusRestoredDefault: Focus: Restored to research default (autofocus disabled).
focusRestoreUnsupported: Focus: Default restore — not supported by this device/driver.
focusRestoreFailed: Focus: Default restore failed. Check camera connection.
focusRestoreNotOpen: Focus: Default restore — camera not yet open. Open preview first.
focusRestoreAcceptedNoReadback: Focus: Default restore accepted, readback unavailable.
autoExposureTooltip: Request auto exposure from the camera. Uncheck to apply a fixed manual exposure value.
manualExposureIndexTooltip: Applied as a relative driver-level value. For precise shutter control use the Environmental Lock, which reads back the actual hardware exposure in seconds.
manualExposureBestEffortNote:
Manual exposure is best-effort. Some cameras may ignore it.
For motion studies, use bright even lighting and fixed exposure.
defaultExposureButtonTooltip: Restore this camera's exposure to its research-safe default (auto exposure off). Clears any manual exposure override.
exposureWarningReadbackUnavailable: Exposure: Request accepted — readback unavailable. Check camera/vendor settings if brightness is inconsistent.
exposureAutoAcceptedNoReadback: Exposure: Auto exposure — request accepted, readback unavailable.
exposureManualSentNoReadback: Exposure: Manual value sent — readback unavailable. Check if preview brightness changed.
exposureRestoredDefault: Exposure: Restored to research default (auto exposure disabled).
exposureRestoreUnsupported: Exposure: Default restore — not supported by this device/driver.
exposureRestoreFailed: Exposure: Default restore failed. Check camera connection.
exposureRestoreNotOpen: Exposure: Default restore — camera not yet open. Open preview first.
exposureRestoreAcceptedNoReadback: Exposure: Default restore accepted, readback unavailable.
whiteBalanceKelvinNote: Kelvin colour temperature (available after Environmental Lock).
environmentalLockDescription: Freezes focus, exposure, white balance and ISO at current hardware values. Press once the arena is lit and the calibration target is in place.
startPreviewToEnableCalibration: Start preview to enable calibration controls.
exposureNotSupported: Exposure: Not supported by this device/driver.
focusNotSupported: Focus: Not supported by this device/driver.
verifyStableTimingNote: Stable timing and continuous real frames are required for scientific analysis.
verifyOriginalCaptureExplanation: Original Capture Mode preserves real camera frames only. Frame counts may differ because cameras delivered real frames at different measured FPS. Real frames only; no duplicates/placeholders.
verifyContainerDurationWarning: Container duration differs from wall-clock time. Use timestamp CSV for scientific trimming and analysis.
verifyStableTimingTooltip: Stable actual FPS, accurate elapsed time, synchronized camera timing, and continuous frames are more important than exact nominal FPS.
verifyFrameIntegrityTooltip: The app records real camera frames only. It does not insert duplicate or placeholder frames. Measured FPS, timestamps, frame counts, and diagnostics are saved in metadata and audit reports.
verifyWriterDropsTooltip: Frames that could not be written because the recording queue became full. Writer drops may affect scientific timing quality.
verifyHardwareCalibrationLockTooltip: Whether the camera hardware parameters (focus, exposure, white balance, ISO) were frozen at recording start for dataset purity.
verifyDeepVerifyTooltip: Independently decode every frame and hash it (MD5) to check for true duplicate frames. Slow — roughly real-time relative to each recording's own duration — and runs only when you click this button.
verifyDeepVerifyExplanation: On-demand only. Decodes every frame and hashes it to independently confirm the duplicate-frame counts reported above are real, not just self-reported by the recorder.
verifyMetadataCompletenessV2Note: N/A — VideoEngineV2 metadata uses a different (fuller) schema; see the VideoEngineV2 section below.
verifyV2SummaryCorrected: [V2] Summary corrected using V2 session audit: {0} videos ({1} pass, {2} warn, {3} fail).
verifyNoteFrameCountDifference: Frame counts may differ because cameras delivered real frames at different measured FPS.
verifyHighConfidenceMessage: Real frames only; no duplicates/placeholders. Use timestamp CSV for timing-sensitive analysis.
verifyV2PreScanDisclaimer: NOTE: The legacy scan below may show FAIL for a VideoEngineV2 session for two different reasons: semantic differences between its exact-match model and V2's frame-counting model (not a real problem), or a genuine discrepancy it independently confirms against real file data (worth reading). Scroll to the end of this log (or check the summary cards) for the reconciled overall result, marked "[V2] Corrected..." — but check each camera's own specific message before assuming a FAIL here is automatically benign.
verifyV2StandaloneComplete: [V2] Standalone verification complete. Pass={0}  Warn={1}  Fail={2}
verifyV2CorrectedFalsePositive: [V2] Reconciled FAIL for session "{0}" -> {1} using the app's own V2 session audit (computed at recording time). This does not mean every check passed — see each camera's own message for any real issue that remains.
verifyV2CorrectedNote: NOTE: Legacy verification reported {0}, but this is a VideoEngineV2 recording — reconciled to {1} using the app's own V2 session audit (computed at recording time), which correctly treats inter-camera frame-count differences from independently-measured real FPS as expected, not a failure. This does not guarantee every check passed — some FAILs below may reflect a real, independently-confirmed issue rather than a schema difference. See per-camera Deep Verify / V2 metadata for the authoritative check.
verifyV2RowCorrectedNote: NOTE: The Result/Failures/Reason/Recommendation shown below come from the legacy verification parser. For a VideoEngineV2 recording, a FAIL here can reflect either a semantic difference from V2's frame-counting model, or a genuine issue the parser independently confirmed against real file data. This camera's reconciled result is {1} (previously shown as {0}) — see the VideoEngineV2 Recording Engine section below for the authoritative data, and check this camera's specific message above before assuming the original FAIL was benign.
verifyV2FrameCountMismatchWarning: Container has {0} real frames but the app's internal counter reported {1} (diff={2}) — camera may have stalled/recovered during recording; verify manually.
verifyMsgNoVideosFound: No MP4 videos found in the selected folder.
verifyMsgStopBoundaryAccepted: One final frame occurred at the stop boundary and was not written. This is accepted and does not indicate frame loss during recording.
verifyMsgOriginalCaptureFramesCapturedDi: Original Capture framesCaptured differs from framesWritten by {0} frame(s)
verifyMsgSessionInterpretation: Original Capture Mode: Real frames only; no duplicates/placeholders. Frame counts may differ because cameras delivered real frames at different measured FPS. Use timestamp CSV for timing-sensitive analysis.
verifyMsgStableFpsBelowRequested: Stable measured FPS below requested FPS is acceptable in Original Capture Mode when timestamps are recorded.
v2CsvMismatchNote: the timestamp CSV recorded more rows than the encoder wrote frames — usually real camera/USB timing jitter (see Timestamp CSV for per-frame gaps), not data loss.
v2WarnBacklightUnsupported: BacklightCompensation not supported on this device.
v2WarnOisUnsupported: OpticalImageStabilizationControl not supported on this device.
v2WarnWhiteBalanceUnsupported: WhiteBalanceControl not supported on this device.
v2WarnControlNotApplied: Control not applied in this build.
v2WarnManagerNotAttached: CameraControlManagerV2 not attached to an open camera session.
v2WarnLlcSkippedExposureUnsupported: Skipped — exposure control unsupported on this device, so disabling low-light compensation would only darken the image with no reproducibility benefit.
v2WarnFlickerNotExposed: Flicker reduction is not exposed by the WinRT camera control API on this system (VideoDeviceController.FlickerReductionControl is absent from all installed Windows SDK metadata) and cannot be applied by MultiCamApp.
v2CsvRowsDiffNote: (timestamp CSV has {0} more row(s) than frames written)
v2IssueTempFileStillExists: Temp file still exists — recording was not finalised. Temp: {0}
v2IssueFinalMp4TooSmall: Final MP4 is very small ({0} bytes) — may be corrupt.
v2IssueTempFileAfterFinalize: Temp file still present after finalisation — rename did not complete.
v2IssueCsvNoDataRows: Timestamp CSV has no data rows (header only or empty).
v2IssueCsvHeaderMissingColumn: Timestamp CSV header does not contain '{0}'.
v2IssueCsvDroppedFramesReported: {0} dropped frame(s) reported in timestamp CSV ({1} total rows).
v2IssueCsvOk: Timestamp CSV has {0} data rows, {1} dropped frames.
verifyUseTimestampCsvForTimingNote: Use timestamp CSV for timing-sensitive analysis.
verifyScanFoundVideosPending: Scan found videos. Detailed verification has not been run yet. Click Verify All to calculate timing, frame integrity, and metadata results.
verifyNoVideoMessage:
No MP4 videos found in the selected folder.

Choose a recording session folder or click Browse.
verifyRecActionNoTimestampCsv: No timestamp CSV available; inspect frame data manually
verifyRecActionReadyNoTimestamp: Ready for analysis; no timestamp CSV was written
verifyColStatusTooltip: Verification status. PASS and PASS_ORIGINAL_TIMING are ready; warnings remain visible.
verifyColRealCaptureFpsTooltip: Measured native camera FPS from capture timestamps.
verifyColTimingSourceTooltip: Recommended source for timing and trimming.
verifyV2StandaloneHeader: VideoEngineV2 Verification (ffprobe not available — V2 metadata only)
verifyV2StandaloneFfprobeNote: Note: Duration, FPS, and codec info require ffprobe. Install ffprobe alongside the app for full verification.
hwDiagPageDescription: Diagnostics are advisory and privacy-safe. They do not change recording behavior.
hwDiagSystemCardPlaceholder: Run a scan to view CPU, RAM, OS, and display adapter status.
hwDiagCameraDevicesCardPlaceholder: Run a scan to list detected camera devices.
hwDiagUsbCardPlaceholder: Run a scan to view advisory USB and camera connection guidance.
hwDiagPrivacyNote: Note: Hardware diagnostics are privacy-safe and do not store hardware IDs or user/computer identifiers.
hwDiagPresetNotChecked: Preset support: not checked by diagnostics
hwDiagFocusControlledByProvider: controlled by provider app or driver
hwDiagPresetAdvisory: Recommended for 3-4 camera 1080p: use separate USB ports or powered USB hub, bright room lighting, preview FPS Balanced, and High-Stability Recording Mode ON.
hwDiagPresetAdvisory1Cam: 1-camera layout: USB bandwidth is rarely a concern. Bright, even room lighting still helps focus and exposure stability.
hwDiagPresetAdvisory2Cam: 2-camera layout: prefer separate USB ports for each camera if available. Bright room lighting and preview FPS Balanced are recommended.
hwDiagNoteAdvisoryOnly: Hardware diagnostics are advisory only. MultiCamApp does not block presets based on this scan.
hwDiagWarnRamUnreadable: RAM information could not be read.
hwDiagWarnDisplayAdapterUnreadable: Display adapter information could not be read.
hwDiagWarnBasicDisplayAdapter: Microsoft Basic Display Adapter detected. Install the official Intel, NVIDIA, or AMD graphics driver for best preview and recording reliability.
hwDiagHintIntel: Intel graphics detected. Official Intel graphics drivers may improve preview stability.
hwDiagHintNvidia: NVIDIA graphics detected. Official NVIDIA drivers may improve display and encoding reliability.
hwDiagHintAmd: AMD/Radeon graphics detected. Official AMD drivers may improve display and encoding reliability.
hwDiagHintNoKnownAdapter: No Intel/NVIDIA/AMD display adapter was identified. This may be normal on some systems.
hwDiagNoteCameraAdvisoryOnly: Camera capability diagnostics are advisory only and do not block recording.
hwDiagNoteNoStressTest: This scanner does not run a long stress test and avoids opening cameras by default.
hwDiagNoteFocusUnavailable: Focus control support is reported as unavailable unless the camera/driver exposes it through generic controls without disturbing active camera use.
hwDiagWarnPresetNotProbed: Not probed to avoid locking cameras or disturbing driver mappings. Unknown does not mean unsupported.
hwDiagWarnVirtualCamera: Virtual camera detected. Availability depends on its provider app and whether another app has locked it.
hwDiagWarnBuiltInCamera: Built-in camera detected. Use 360p first; use 720p only if stable. Do not use built-in fallback for 1080p stress.
hwDiagWarnNoCamerasToScan: No cameras were available to scan.
hwDiagUsbAdvisoryFor34Cam: USB topology advisory is most useful for 3/4-camera layouts.
hwDiagUsbAdvisoryOnlyNote: USB topology diagnostics are advisory only and do not change selected devices, opening order, or recording behavior.
hwDiagScanComplete: Hardware scan complete. Results are advisory only.
hwDiagNoScanYet: No hardware scan has been run yet.
hwDiagBasicAdapterBody: Microsoft Basic Display Adapter was detected. Install the official Intel, NVIDIA, or AMD graphics driver for best preview and recording reliability.
languageLockedDuringRecordingTooltip: Language cannot be changed during recording.
experimentConstantFrameCountTooltip: Measured FPS and timestamps are saved in metadata.
videoSettingsRestartHint: Stop preview and start again to apply new resolution or FPS.
recordSettingsMismatchIntro:
The resolution/FPS in Video Settings do not match what the cameras are capturing yet.

Wait for settings to apply, or stop preview and start again.

Record at the current capture size anyway?
duplicateDeviceWarning: Each camera slot must use a different device. Select another camera for Cam 3 / Cam 4, or connect more cameras.
duplicateDeviceInSlot: This camera is already selected in cam{0}. Please choose a different device for cam{1}.
previewPartial: Preview active on {0}/{1} camera(s). Some slots failed to open.
previewMissingDevice: One or more selected cameras are unavailable. Click Refresh cameras or choose another device.
previewSlotTryLowerPreset360: Check camera connection or choose another camera.
previewSlotTryLowerPresetGeneric: Check camera connection or selected preset.
recordingSlotNotReady: Cannot start recording because {0} is not ready.
deviceChangeWhileRecording: Stop recording before changing camera devices.
recordingSlotFailed: {0} failed to start recording. Recording was cancelled safely. Please check camera support, USB bandwidth, or selected resolution.
recordingFirstFrameTimeout: {0} did not write a frame within {1} seconds. Recording was cancelled safely. Please check camera support, USB bandwidth, or selected resolution.
licenseUsageBody:
MultiCamApp may be used free of charge for non-commercial academic research,
scientific work, education, laboratory experiments, reproducibility studies,
and evaluation.
commercialUseBody:
Commercial use, resale, sublicensing, SaaS hosting, or monetized redistribution
requires written permission from the copyright holder.
attributionKoketsuTitle: Original prototype workflow concept and foundational recording logic:
attributionAungTitle:
Modern application architecture, UI/UX redesign, offline packaging,
verification framework, diagnostics, metadata/reporting, localization,
testing, release engineering, and maintenance:
thirdPartyNoticesBody: This application includes third-party components. After installation, see THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md and runtime/ffmpeg/FFMPEG_LICENSE.txt for license, attribution, and source-code reference information.
multiCamUsbBandwidthWarning: Using 3 or 4 USB cameras may require enough USB bandwidth. If recording fails at 1080p, try 720p or 360p, use separate USB ports/controllers, or reduce the camera count.
previewFailed: Could not open camera. Try Refresh cameras or another device.
cameraAccessBlocked: Camera access is blocked by Windows privacy settings. Please enable camera access for MultiCamApp in Windows Settings > Privacy & security > Camera.
cameraRefreshMissingDevices: Camera refresh complete. One or more selected cameras are unavailable.
verifyPageSubtitle: Stable timing and continuous real frames are required for scientific analysis.
verifySelectFolder: Please select a recording folder first.
verifyNoVideos: No MP4 videos found in the selected folder.
verifyFfprobeMissing: Video verification tool is missing. Please reinstall MultiCamApp or check runtime/ffmpeg.
verifyFfmpegMissing: Deep Verify tool (ffmpeg) is missing. Please reinstall MultiCamApp or check runtime/ffmpeg.
verifyDeepVerifyStarted: Deep Verify started for {0} file(s). This may take a while.
verifyNothingToExport: Run verification before exporting a report.
verifyDetailEmpty: Select a camera row within a session to view detailed verification results.
verifyTimingInterpretation1: Videos marked PASS are valid for frame-based analysis.
verifyTimingInterpretation2: Container duration (ffprobe) uses the MP4 FPS tag; wall-clock duration is the actual recording time.
verifyTimingInterpretation3: PASS_WITH_WARNING often means the camera delivered ~29 fps while the file is tagged 30 fps — expected, not corruption.
verifyTimingInterpretation4: Use frame count and wall-clock timing for scientific analysis, not ffprobe duration alone.
verifySessionScopeNote: Inter-camera comparison is performed only within the same recording session. Videos from different sessions are audited individually but not compared with each other.
verifyScanComplete: Scan complete: {0} session(s), {1} camera video(s) found.
startupCouldNotStart: MultiCamApp could not start. Check the log file below for details, or reinstall the application.
startupConfigMissing: Configuration or localisation files are missing. Please reinstall the application.
dialogAboutOpenError:
Could not open the About window.

{0}
reportSessionSummaryHeader: Session	Status	Cameras	FrameDiff	StartOffsetMs	Drops	Duplicates
reportInterpretation1: PASS and PASS_WITH_WARNING videos are valid for frame-based analysis.
reportInterpretation2: Container duration reflects the MP4 FPS tag; wall-clock duration is the actual recording time.
reportInterpretation3: PASS_WITH_WARNING often means camera delivery (~29 fps) differs from the 30 fps container tag.
reportInterpretation4: Use frame count and wall-clock timing for scientific analysis, not ffprobe duration alone.
reportExportEnglishByDesignNote: Note: PASS / PASS_WITH_WARNING / FAIL, cam folder names (cam1–cam4), CSV column identifiers, ffprobe field names (e.g. pix_fmt), and engine diagnostic lines are kept in English to match metadata and analysis tools.
metaScientificTimingDefaultMessage: Video is valid. Camera delivery FPS may differ from MP4 container FPS. For scientific analysis, use frame count and wall-clock/monotonic timing instead of ffprobe duration alone.
metaCaptureIntervalUnavailable: Insufficient capture timestamps for interval statistics.
verifyMsgMetadataJsonUnparsed: {0}: metadata.json present but could not be parsed
metaTimingStatusLine: Real Capture FPS: {0}; Playback FPS: {1}; Container vs wall-clock: {2}s; Status: {3}
metaTimingGuidance1: Video is valid for frame-based analysis when scientific timing status is PASS or PASS_WITH_WARNING.
metaTimingGuidance2: Container duration (ffprobe) is frame-based; wall-clock duration is the real recording time.
metaTimingGuidance3: Container vs wall-clock difference is expected when camera delivery (~29 fps) differs from the 30 fps MP4 tag.
metaTimingGuidance4: Use frame count and wall-clock/monotonic timing for scientific analysis, not ffprobe duration alone.
verifyRecVideoMissing: Video file or stream is missing; re-record or copy the file into the session folder.
verifyRecResolutionMismatch: Resolution does not match expected settings; check camera mode and metadata.
verifyRecReviewFailures: Review failure messages and re-record if the file is unusable.
verifyRecDurationFfmpeg: Check per-camera metadata writing accuracy; ffprobe duration may differ slightly from writer timing.
verifyRecFpsOff: FPS differs from requested rate; confirm capture settings and camera capability.
verifyRecMetadataMissing: Add or restore metadata.txt for full verification against expected settings.
verifyRecReviewWarnings: Video is readable; review warnings before using for analysis.
verifyRecTimingFailed: Timing integrity failed; rely on the frame-based metadata or re-record the session.
verifyRecNoAction: Video matches expected settings within tolerance; no action required.
sessionAuditPipelineIntegrity: Pipeline integrity issue: drops={0}, duplicates={1}, placeholders={2}
sessionAuditConstantFrameCount: Constant frame count sync inserted duplicate frames: duplicates={0}, placeholders={1}
sessionAuditResolutionMismatch: Resolution mismatch between cameras in this session
sessionAuditCodecMismatch: Codec mismatch between cameras in this session
sessionAuditPixelFormatMismatch: Pixel format mismatch between cameras in this session
sessionAuditFrameDiffTooLarge: Inter-camera frame difference too large ({0} frames)
sessionAuditStartOffsetTooLarge: Inter-camera start offset too large ({0:F1} ms)
sessionAuditStopOffsetTooLarge: Inter-camera stop offset too large ({0:F1} ms)
sessionAuditSingleCameraNote: Single-camera session: inter-camera comparison not applicable.
sessionAuditFramesCapturedDiff: {0}: FramesCaptured differs from FramesWritten by 1 frame
sessionAuditFpsContainerMeasuredDiff: {0}: container/writer FPS differs from measured camera FPS
sessionAuditOriginalCaptureDuplicates: Original Capture Mode expected duplicateFrames=0, but duplicateFrames={0}. Re-record before scientific use.
sessionAuditOriginalFrameDiffInfo: Frame counts may differ because cameras delivered real frames at different measured FPS. Difference: {0} frame(s).
